title: Liber AL vel Legis
author: Aleister Crowley
description: The received text that opens Crowley's Thelemic system and frames the Cairo Working. It is the portal's primary doctrinal starting point and the source for Nuit, Hadit, Aiwass, and True Will. The document anchors the bibliography for Liber AL vel Legis, keeping the source lane visible alongside the works and events it supports. The portal treats documents as evidence-bearing companions rather than simple attachments, so the description explains why the text matters to the archive.

title: 777 and Other Qabalistic Writings
author: Aleister Crowley
description: Crowley's correspondence tables and Qabalistic reference work. The book is less a narrative than a lookup engine for tarot, astrology, deity names, and the symbolic cross-links that make his system navigable. The document anchors the bibliography for 777 and Other Qabalistic Writings, keeping the source lane visible alongside the works and events it supports. The portal treats documents as evidence-bearing companions rather than simple attachments, so the description explains why the text matters to the archive.

title: The Book of Thoth
author: Aleister Crowley
description: Crowley's late tarot manual for the Thoth deck and its symbolic revisions. It is where he explains the deck, defends his attributions, and clarifies the symbolic logic behind the Thoth design. The document anchors the bibliography for The Book of Thoth, keeping the source lane visible alongside the works and events it supports. The portal treats documents as evidence-bearing companions rather than simple attachments, so the description explains why the text matters to the archive.

title: The Equinox, Vol. I, No. 1
author: Aleister Crowley
description: The first issue of Crowley's magical journal, with instruction, ritual, and polemic. It establishes the periodical voice that lets him publish doctrine, training material, and self-curated evidence side by side. The document anchors the bibliography for The Equinox, Vol. I, No. 1, keeping the source lane visible alongside the works and events it supports. The portal treats documents as evidence-bearing companions rather than simple attachments, so the description explains why the text matters to the archive.

title: The Book of Lies
author: Aleister Crowley
description: Short symbolic chapters, jokes, aphorisms, and doctrinal reversals. The text rewards close reading because its comedy is also a method for compressing doctrine and testing the reader's interpretive discipline. The document anchors the bibliography for The Book of Lies, keeping the source lane visible alongside the works and events it supports. The portal treats documents as evidence-bearing companions rather than simple attachments, so the description explains why the text matters to the archive.

title: Liber Aleph vel CXI
author: Aleister Crowley
description: Crowley's letter-form work of magical instruction addressed to his magical son. It mixes ethical counsel, occult pedagogy, and intimate self-presentation, making the voice feel both paternal and ceremonial. The document anchors the bibliography for Liber Aleph vel CXI, keeping the source lane visible alongside the works and events it supports. title: The Blue Equinox
author: Aleister Crowley
description: Later issue of The Equinox and a major post-war manifesto volume. It gathers doctrine, ritual, and polemical framing from the New York years and bridges the exile period to the Abbey experiment. The document anchors the bibliography for The Blue Equinox, keeping the source lane visible alongside the works and events it supports. title: Magick in Theory and Practice
author: Aleister Crowley
description: A major statement of Crowley's magical theory and practical method. The book translates his system into more direct instructional prose and remains one of the clearest entry points into his mature magical method. The document anchors the bibliography for Magick in Theory and Practice, keeping the source lane visible alongside the works and events it supports. title: The Confessions of Aleister Crowley
author: Aleister Crowley
description: Crowley's autobiographical life story, written as a self-interpreting narrative. It is indispensable for the archive, but its value comes from the way it reveals how Crowley wanted the record of his life to look. The document anchors the bibliography for The Confessions of Aleister Crowley, keeping the source lane visible alongside the works and events it supports. The portal treats documents as evidence-bearing companions rather than simple attachments, so the description explains why the text matters to the archive.

title: Diary of a Drug Fiend
author: Aleister Crowley
description: Crowley's famous novel of discipline, addiction, and transformation. It dramatizes the struggle between compulsion and structure in a way that makes the Abbey years and his therapeutic rhetoric easier to read. The document anchors the bibliography for Diary of a Drug Fiend, keeping the source lane visible alongside the works and events it supports.
